_comment: KIA connectors — same shape as Claude's MCP config. Copy to data/connectors.json, fill secrets, set CONNECTORS_ENABLED=true. Each server is an MCP server launched over stdio. Install servers via npx/uvx as shown. Remove any server you don't use so KIA doesn't try to launch it.

fetch._comment: Fetch & read arbitrary web pages as markdown. Python server via uvx — pairs with web-search (search -> fetch the top result).

postgres._comment: Read-only SQL access. Swap the connection string for your DB. For SQLite instead use: command npx, args ['-y','@modelcontextprotocol/server-sqlite','/app/data/kia.db'].

memory._comment: Persistent knowledge-graph memory the model can write entities/relations to. Survives restarts via MEMORY_FILE_PATH.

notion._comment: Notion docs/pages. Create an internal integration at notion.so/my-integrations, share the pages with it, paste the secret below.

_note_github: @modelcontextprotocol/server-github is deprecated on npm but still functional. For long-term use, consider GitHub's official MCP server (github.com/github/github-mcp-server, a Go/Docker binary) — add it as a command entry pointing at the binary.
_note_gdrive: Google Drive MCP needs OAuth: clone github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers, build packages/gdrive, run its auth flow to mint a credentials file, then add a 'gdrive' entry pointing at the built dist with GDRIVE_CREDENTIALS_PATH. It can't be a one-line npx like the others, which is why it's documented here rather than enabled above.
